domain: Ainu yukar rhythmic epic recitation (Hokkaido oral tradition; reciter + listeners both tap rhythm; listeners interject at story-development points; rhythm is structural validation that recitation is on-track)
specific_mechanism: Audience-side rhythm tapping serves as continuous validation gate; falling off rhythm signals memory failure / mis-recitation; structural refrains anchor cadence.
llm_application: YUKAR-RHYTHM-GATE: a runtime cadence-validation gate for LLM generation — (i) at fixed K-token intervals during generation, a lightweight cadence-classifier computes the model's current sentence-length rhythm, function-word frequency, and structural-marker density; (ii) compares to a TARGET CADENCE learned from clean/in-distribution training data; (iii) if deviation exceeds threshold ε, triggers EARLY-STOP or BACKUP-TO-CHECKPOINT. Different from semantic validators (meaning) and format validators (schema) — this is PROSODY-LEVEL temporal validation. Hallucination correlates with cadence drift (model 'falls off rhythm').
novelty_claim: Constrained-decoding enforces format via grammar. Semantic validators check meaning. Self-consistency votes across samples. Confidence-based early-stop uses logit entropy. YUKAR-RHYTHM-GATE differs by PROSODY/CADENCE-LEVEL validation (sentence-length distribution + function-word + structural-marker frequency) at FIXED K-TOKEN intervals with EARLY-STOP/BACKUP-CHECKPOINT response. The cadence is the diagnostic signal.
